metadata.name: IE->UK Dairy & Beef Export Corridor (W4M-WWG) -- Instance Data
metadata.domain: International Dairy & Beef Trade / Cold Chain Logistics
metadata.description: Complete graph of the Cork processing plant -> Dublin Port -> Irish Sea ferry -> Holyhead/Liverpool -> UK distribution corridor for Irish dairy (butter, cheese, cream) and beef (chilled prime cuts). Simplest and shortest corridor in the WWG portfolio: 1-3 day transit, 3-8 hour Irish Sea crossing. Highest volume (12,000 tonnes/year). Lowest risk score (3.1). Unique Windsor Framework regulatory dimension introduces political/regulatory uncertainty not present in other corridors. Includes 5 chain nodes, 3 legs, 7 parties, 5 compliance gates, 8 trade documents, 5 bottlenecks with risk assessments, 3 hypotheses, 2 scenarios, 3 cascade effects, 2 predictive patterns, 1 example shipment.

examples.name: Irish Dairy & Beef -> UK Retail/Wholesale
examples.description: Chilled Irish dairy (Kerrygold butter, cheddar, cream) and beef (chilled prime cuts from ABP Food Group) from Cork processing plants to UK wholesale and retail distribution via Dublin Port, Irish Sea ferry to Holyhead or Liverpool. 1-3 day total transit -- shortest corridor in WWG portfolio. Windsor Framework 2023 provides simplified GB market access for goods moving from Ireland, but political uncertainty around future regulatory alignment creates unique risk. Highest volume corridor: 12,000 tonnes/year across approximately 500 trailer loads. DAP Daventry terms.
examples.commodity: Irish dairy (butter, cheese, cream) and beef (chilled prime cuts)
examples.regulatoryRegime: Windsor Framework 2023; IE DAFM Export Requirements; UK BTOM 2024; Northern Ireland Protocol

examples.name: Cork Processing Plant (Dairy & Beef)
examples.capabilities: dairy-processing, butter-packing, cheese-cutting, beef-boning, vacuum-pack, blast-chill, DAFM-approved, Bord Bia Quality Assured

examples.name: Dublin Port -- Ferry Terminal
examples.location: Dublin Port, Alexandra Road, Dublin 1, Ireland
examples.operator: Dublin Port Company / Irish Ferries / Stena Line

examples.name: Holyhead Ferry Port (or Liverpool Birkenhead)
examples.operator: Stena Line Ports Ltd / Irish Ferries UK

examples.name: Daventry WWG Cold Store (National Distribution Centre)
examples.location: DIRFT (Daventry International Rail Freight Terminal), Northamptonshire
examples.capabilities: 0-4C holding, multi-customer, pick-and-pack, retail-ready-packaging, stock-rotation, dairy-segregation

examples.name: Customer Delivery Point (UK Retailer / Wholesale)
examples.location: Various UK (supermarket RDCs, wholesale depots, food service)

examples.distance: 260 km (Cork to Dublin Port via M8/M7)

examples.vesselName: MV Ulysses (Irish Ferries) or Stena Adventurer
examples.route: Dublin Port then Irish Sea then Holyhead (or Liverpool Birkenhead)

examples.name: Ornua Co-operative (Kerrygold)
examples.role: Irish dairy processor and exporter -- butter, cheese, cream production and export
examples.responsibilities: Product quality, DAFM compliance, health certificate application, trailer loading, shelf-life guarantee, Bord Bia quality mark
examples.riskOwnership: Product quality to FCA Cork. Cold chain to FCA. Regulatory compliance (IE side).
examples.licences: DAFM Approved Establishment IE-CK-0412, Bord Bia Origin Green Certified, BRC Grade AA

examples.name: ABP Food Group (Irish Division)
examples.role: Irish beef processor and exporter -- slaughter, boning, vacuum-pack, chilled prime cuts
examples.responsibilities: Product quality, DAFM compliance, health certificate application, Bord Bia Quality Assured beef, trailer loading
examples.riskOwnership: Product quality to FCA. Cold chain to dispatch. Regulatory compliance (IE side).
examples.licences: DAFM Approved Establishment IE-CK-0187, Bord Bia Sustainable Beef & Lamb Assurance, BRC Grade A

examples.name: WWG UK Import & Distribution Ltd
examples.role: UK importer of record -- customs clearance, GVMS pre-lodgement, BCP liaison, cold storage, distribution
examples.responsibilities: UK import compliance, GVMS pre-lodgement, IPAFFS notification (where required), duty payment, cold storage, customer delivery
examples.riskOwnership: All risk from DAP Holyhead/Liverpool. UK regulatory compliance. Storage and distribution costs.
examples.licences: HMRC EORI GB-742819365000, GVMS Registered Haulier, DEFRA Approved Importer

examples.name: Irish Continental Group plc (Irish Ferries / Stena Line)
examples.role: Irish Sea ferry operator -- ro-ro freight service Dublin to Holyhead/Liverpool
examples.responsibilities: Vessel operation, trailer boarding, temperature maintenance during crossing, on-time arrival, sailing schedule reliability
examples.riskOwnership: Carrier liability during crossing. Force majeure for weather cancellations (standard ferry terms).

examples.name: O'Reilly Customs & Trade Compliance Ltd
examples.role: Irish customs agent -- export declarations, DAFM liaison, Windsor Framework documentation
examples.responsibilities: Export customs declaration, DAFM health cert coordination, Windsor Framework documentation, origin certification
examples.riskOwnership: Declaration accuracy. Penalty liability for misclassification.
examples.licences: Irish Revenue Authorised Customs Agent CA-IE-2847

examples.name: DAFM (Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine)
examples.role: Irish export regulatory authority -- food safety certification, establishment approval, veterinary health certification
examples.responsibilities: Health certificate issuance, export establishment approval, veterinary inspection, residue testing, Bord Bia scheme oversight
examples.riskOwnership: Regulatory authority -- sets the rules. Non-negotiable.
examples.contactSLA: Government SLA (2-5 business days standard, 24 hours urgent)

examples.name: APHA (Animal & Plant Health Agency)
examples.role: UK border SPS authority -- import inspection, health certificate verification, Windsor Framework compliance checks
examples.responsibilities: CHED-P verification (where applicable), identity checks, documentary checks, Windsor Framework risk-based controls, laboratory sampling
examples.riskOwnership: Regulatory authority -- can reject, detain, or destroy non-compliant consignments.

examples.name: DAFM Health Certificate & Export Certification
examples.commonFailureReasons: Residue test positive, document mismatch between health cert and commercial invoice, establishment audit finding

examples.name: Irish Export Customs Clearance
examples.avgDuration: 2-4 hours (largely automated for IE->GB Windsor Framework goods)
examples.commonFailureReasons: Tariff classification error, incomplete declaration, sanctions screening flag

examples.name: UK Entry -- Windsor Framework Simplified Controls
examples.authority: HMRC via GVMS (Goods Vehicle Movement Service)
examples.avgDuration: 30 minutes - 2 hours (GVMS green lane for Windsor Framework qualifying goods)
examples.commonFailureReasons: GVMS pre-lodgement error, Windsor Framework qualification disputed (product not clearly IE-origin), system outage

examples.name: BCP Document Check (Risk-Based under Windsor Framework)
examples.avgDuration: 1-4 hours (only if selected for risk-based check)
examples.commonFailureReasons: Health cert / commercial invoice mismatch, trailer seal discrepancy, temperature logger anomaly

examples.name: UK Import Customs Clearance (CDS)
examples.avgDuration: 1-4 hours (simplified under Windsor Framework -- most auto-cleared)
examples.commonFailureReasons: Incorrect tariff code, Windsor Framework preference not properly claimed, duty underpayment on non-qualifying goods

examples.issuingAuthority: HMRC via Goods Vehicle Movement Service
Trailer data logger (Carrier Transicold / Thermo King)

examples._section: === BOTTLENECKS (The Tight Spots) with Risk + Net Impact ===

examples.name: Windsor Framework Regulatory Uncertainty
examples.description: The Windsor Framework (2023) provides simplified GB market access for goods moving from Ireland, reducing SPS checks and customs friction. However, the Framework is politically sensitive: UK government policy changes, EU-UK Joint Committee decisions, or DUP/unionist opposition could tighten rules. Any change to Windsor Framework green/red lane classifications directly impacts this corridor's speed advantage.
examples.typicalDelay: Unknown (0 days if status quo; 1-5 days if controls tightened)
examples.seasonality: Event-driven -- political cycle, UK election, EU-UK review points
examples.rootCause: Political sensitivity of Northern Ireland Protocol; EU-UK regulatory divergence; UK domestic political dynamics
examples.mitigationStrategy: Maintain dual documentation capability (Windsor simplified + full BTOM); engage with Dairy Industry Ireland and IBEC lobbying; monitor EU-UK Joint Committee announcements
examples.workaroundDescription: If Windsor Framework tightened: revert to full BTOM documentation process, adding 1-3 days. Alternative: route via Northern Ireland land bridge for NI-specific simplified access.

examples.name: Northern Ireland Land Bridge Complexity
examples.description: Alternative route IE->NI (land border)->GB ferry avoids direct IE->GB customs friction but introduces NI Protocol complexity. Goods transiting NI must comply with both EU and UK rules. Documentation, customs status (not-at-risk vs at-risk determination), and transit declarations add administrative burden. Used as fallback when direct IE->GB faces Windsor Framework challenges.
examples.typicalDelay: 1-2 days additional (NI transit documentation + Belfast ferry)
examples.seasonality: Not seasonal -- triggered by regulatory events
examples.rootCause: Dual regulatory zone (NI Protocol); not-at-risk goods determination complexity; additional customs transit declarations
examples.mitigationStrategy: Pre-authorised trusted trader status for NI transit; dedicated NI customs broker; maintain NI transit documentation templates
examples.workaroundDescription: Revert to direct IE->GB routing and accept full BTOM controls rather than NI land bridge complexity.

examples.name: Irish Sea Ferry Capacity & Weather Cancellations
examples.description: Irish Sea ferry services (Irish Ferries, Stena Line) operate finite ro-ro capacity. Peak periods (Christmas, bank holidays, agricultural show season) see 95-100% freight bookings. Weather cancellations (Irish Sea storms, fog) cause 3-8 sailing cancellations per winter, leading to trailer backlogs of 200-400 units at Dublin Port. Unlike deep-sea shipping, no alternative route -- all IE->GB freight funnels through Irish Sea.
examples.seasonality: Oct-Mar peak (Irish Sea storms); Dec peak (Christmas freight surge)
examples.rootCause: Geographic chokepoint; limited ferry operator competition; weather vulnerability; finite vessel capacity
examples.mitigationStrategy: Book priority freight slots 2 weeks ahead; maintain relationships with both Irish Ferries and Stena; Rosslare-Fishguard as alternative route
examples.workaroundDescription: Switch to Rosslare-Fishguard route (3.5 hours); or Rosslare-Pembroke (4 hours); or Cork-Roscoff-UK (longer but avoids Irish Sea entirely).

examples.name: DAFM Certification Backlogs (Seasonal)
examples.description: DAFM veterinary certification capacity is finite. During peak dairy season (May-September) and pre-Christmas beef export surge, health certificate processing times extend from 1-2 days to 3-5 days. Cork regional offices particularly affected. Dual-use products (dairy+beef) require separate certifications.
examples.rootCause: Finite DAFM veterinary inspector capacity; seasonal volume spikes; dual certification requirement for mixed loads
examples.mitigationStrategy: Submit certification applications 5+ business days ahead during peak; maintain standing export approval; separate dairy and beef loads where possible

examples.name: Short-Haul Minimal Buffer -- Any Delay Immediately Impacts Delivery Window
examples.description: With only 1-3 day total transit time, any delay immediately consumes the delivery window. Unlike long-haul corridors where a 1-day delay is absorbed within a 35-40 day transit, a 1-day delay in this corridor represents 33-100% of total transit time. Ferry cancellation (12-36 hours) can cause a missed customer delivery slot, leading to direct customer penalty or stock-out at retail level.
examples.rootCause: Structural: short transit = zero buffer. Just-in-time UK retail supply chains assume reliable 1-2 day IE->UK transit.
examples.mitigationStrategy: Build 1-day buffer into customer delivery commitments; maintain 2-day safety stock at Daventry DC; pre-position for critical customers
examples.workaroundDescription: Emergency road haulage via NI land bridge (Dublin-Belfast-Cairnryan-M6) if Dublin-Holyhead ferry cancelled. Adds 6-8 hours but avoids ferry dependency.

examples.regulatoryConsequence: If Windsor Framework tightened, ALL IE->GB food exporters affected; industry-wide cost increase; potential trade diversion to EU markets
examples.cascadeEffect: Regulatory change forces documentation rework across all shipments; delivery delays until new process bedded in; customer confidence drop
Missed customer delivery slot; penalty clause GBP 500-2,000; stock-out at retail for 24-48 hours
Trailer sits loaded at Cork; refrigeration cost EUR 80/day; missed ferry booking may incur rebooking fee
Customer penalty + potential delisting if repeated; emergency road reroute cost GBP 800-1,500; reputational damage with buyer
Additional customs documentation cost; NI transit delay; Belfast ferry availability uncertainty

examples.statement: The Windsor Framework simplified controls for IE->GB food exports will remain materially unchanged through 2028, with green lane processing maintaining sub-2-hour clearance times for qualifying dairy and beef products
examples.baselineValue: Current green lane clearance: 30 min - 2 hours (2024-2025 average)
examples.confidenceInterval: Wide -- 30 min to 5 days depending on political scenario
examples.measurementMethod: Average GVMS clearance time at Holyhead/Liverpool for IE-origin dairy and beef; Windsor Framework Joint Committee decisions
examples.dataSource: HMRC GVMS performance data; EU-UK Joint Committee minutes; DEFRA/DAFM bilateral reports
examples.actualOutcome: 2024-2025: green lane operational, average 45 minutes, no material changes announced
examples.variance: Performing well vs baseline; political signals mixed
examples.learningNote: Windsor Framework stability depends on UK government composition and EU-UK relations. 2025 UK general election maintained status quo. Any future government seeking closer EU alignment could simplify further; divergence-seeking government could complicate.
examples.nextIteration: Monitor 2026 EU-UK Joint Committee review; track DUP/unionist legal challenges; assess any UK regulatory divergence proposals

examples.statement: Irish dairy export volume to UK peaks May-September at 40% above winter baseline, driven by grass-fed production cycle and Kerrygold seasonal butter manufacturing. This creates predictable 3-month capacity requirement surges on this corridor.
examples.baselineValue: Winter baseline: 800 tonnes/month (Dec-Mar). Summer peak: 1,100-1,200 tonnes/month (May-Sep)
examples.confidenceInterval: 35-50% uplift (correlated with grass growing season rainfall)
examples.measurementMethod: Monthly dispatch tonnage from Cork plant to Dublin Port; ferry booking data
examples.dataSource: Ornua dispatch records; CSO (Central Statistics Office) dairy export data; Met Eireann rainfall
examples.actualOutcome: 2025: 42% summer uplift; peak July at 1,180 tonnes. Dry June reduced cream volume slightly.
examples.learningNote: Irish dairy is fundamentally grass-fed and seasonal. Climate change extending grass season slightly (April start becoming common). Kerrygold butter production tightly linked to spring grass flush.
examples.nextIteration: Correlate with Met Eireann seasonal outlook for 2026; model climate-change extended season impact on corridor capacity requirements

examples.statement: Irish Sea ferry service reliability (on-time departure within 2 hours of schedule) will average 88% annually, dropping to 75% during Oct-Mar storm season, with 3-8 full cancellation events per winter
examples.baselineValue: 2023-2025 average: 87% annual reliability; 73% Oct-Mar; 5 cancellation events per winter
examples.measurementMethod: Ferry operator on-time performance data; Dublin Port freight throughput stats; Met Eireann marine forecasts
examples.dataSource: Irish Ferries/Stena Line published service data; Irish Maritime Development Office; Dublin Port freight stats
examples.actualOutcome: 2024-25 winter: 71% Oct-Mar reliability (3 named storms); 6 cancellation events; annual 2025: 86%
examples.variance: -2% annual, -4% winter vs predicted; Storm Eowyn and Storm Herminia both caused 36+ hour closures
examples.learningNote: Irish Sea weather volatility may be increasing (climate change signal, though small sample). New Irish Ferries vessel (W.B. Yeats) handles heavier seas than older fleet -- fleet renewal may improve winter reliability. Rosslare-Fishguard gaining share as Dublin-Holyhead backup.
examples.nextIteration: Factor in new vessel fleet reliability data; assess Rosslare-Fishguard as systematic alternative rather than ad-hoc backup

examples.name: Windsor Framework Rule Change -- GB Market Access Controls Tightened
examples.triggerCondition: UK government announces that Windsor Framework green lane will require full SPS documentation for dairy/meat products, effectively reverting to standard BTOM controls
examples.assumptions: UK regulatory divergence from EU on food standards; political pressure from UK farming lobby; EU retaliation on non-compliance
examples.transitTimeChange: +1-3 days (full BTOM process including IPAFFS pre-notification, BCP physical checks, CHED-P submission)
examples.costChange: +EUR 1,500-3,000 per consignment in documentation, BCP fees, and demurrage
examples.shelfLifeImpact: 1-3 days consumed by new controls -- significant for short-shelf-life dairy (cream, soft cheese)
examples.mitigationPlan: Maintain full BTOM documentation capability as dormant process; lobby via Dairy Industry Ireland and IBEC; explore NI land bridge routing; assess EU market diversion for most affected products (cream, soft cheese)
examples.predictiveSignals: EU-UK Joint Committee agenda items; DEFRA consultation papers on SPS regime; UK food standards divergence proposals; DUP legal actions on Windsor Framework

examples.name: Irish Sea Severe Weather Closure -- 48+ Hours All Sailings Cancelled
examples.triggerCondition: Met Eireann red weather warning for Irish Sea; sustained winds >60 knots for 48+ hours; all Dublin-Holyhead and Dublin-Liverpool sailings cancelled
examples.assumptions: No alternative sea route available; road via NI land bridge possible but congested; airlines not viable for bulk freight
examples.shelfLifeImpact: 2-4 days consumed -- critical for cream (7-day shelf) and soft cheese
examples.mitigationPlan: Pre-position 3 days buffer stock at Daventry DC from October; activate NI land bridge routing (Dublin-Belfast-Cairnryan-M74-M6) as automatic failover; Rosslare-Pembroke as secondary ferry option (more sheltered approach); priority rebooking contracts with Irish Ferries
examples.predictiveSignals: Met Eireann 5-day marine forecast; named storm warnings; ferry operator pre-cancellation notices (typically 12-24 hours ahead)

examples.name: Regulatory Change leads to Documentation Rework leads to Delivery Delay
examples.description: Any Windsor Framework tightening forces immediate documentation rework across ALL shipments in the pipeline. Staff must be retrained on BTOM process. GVMS pre-lodgement templates must be rebuilt. IPAFFS registration required. First 4-6 weeks see 20-30% error rates on new documentation, causing holds at UK border. Domino effect: Cork dispatch delays (waiting for corrected certs) -> missed ferry slots -> customer delivery failures -> penalty clauses -> potential delisting by UK retailers.
examples.triggerDelay: Immediate on announcement; 4-6 weeks transition pain
examples.affectedParties: ALL IE->GB food exporters (industry-wide); importers (new compliance burden); customs agents (process rebuild); UK retailers (supply disruption)
examples.preventionAction: Maintain dormant BTOM-ready documentation process; annual staff refresher on full BTOM; pre-built IPAFFS templates; engage in EU-UK Joint Committee consultation

examples.name: Ferry Cancellation leads to Road Reroute via NI Land Bridge
examples.description: When Dublin-Holyhead/Liverpool ferries cancelled (weather/capacity), freight diverts to NI land bridge: Dublin -> M1 -> Belfast -> Cairnryan ferry -> M74/M6 -> Daventry. This adds 8-12 hours total but introduces NI Protocol transit complexity. Hundreds of trailers competing for Belfast-Cairnryan/Larne-Cairnryan capacity. Customs transit declarations required for NI->GB movement. If many IE exporters divert simultaneously, Belfast ferry capacity overwhelmed, creating secondary bottleneck.
examples.triggerDelay: Immediate -- rerouting decision within 4 hours of cancellation
examples.affectedParties: WWG (rerouting cost + time); NI ferry operators (surge demand); Belfast customs (increased declarations); other NI freight users (displaced capacity)
examples.preventionAction: Cannot prevent weather. Pre-position stock at Daventry to absorb 48-hour disruption; pre-arrange Belfast ferry priority booking; maintain NI transit documentation readiness.

examples.name: Irish Dairy Seasonal Peak leads to Corridor Capacity Crunch
examples.description: May-September Irish grass-fed dairy production peaks: butter +50%, cheese +30%, cream +60% vs winter baseline. ALL Irish dairy exporters surge simultaneously. Cork-Dublin road congested with reefer trailers. Dublin Port freight berths oversubscribed. Irish Ferries/Stena add supplementary sailings but capacity maxes out. Beef exporters (also peaking pre-summer) compete for same ferry slots. Combined effect: booking lead times extend from 2 days to 7-10 days; spot freight rates +20-30%; occasional trailer stranding at Dublin Port 24-48 hours.
examples.triggerDelay: Gradual build from April; peak pressure Jun-Aug
examples.affectedParties: ALL IE dairy/beef exporters; ferry operators (capacity constraint); Dublin Port (congestion); UK importers (delivery variability)
examples.preventionAction: 12-month ferry capacity contract with guaranteed slots; pre-book Jun-Aug sailings by March; Rosslare-Fishguard as overflow route; consider Cork-Swansea direct ferry (seasonal service); spread dispatches evenly through week

examples.name: Irish Dairy Production Season (May-Sep Grass Flush)
examples.description: Irish dairy production follows grass growth cycle. Cows turned out to pasture mid-March; peak milk production May-July; butter/cheese manufacturing peaks 4-6 weeks later. Export volume to UK peaks June-September. Kerrygold butter manufacturing runs 24/7 May-August. Pattern is fundamentally biological and highly predictable -- only extreme drought or extended winter disrupts.
examples.leadIndicators: Met Eireann spring rainfall/temperature; Teagasc grass growth model; ICBF milk recording data; Ornua production forecasts
examples.typicalImpact: Volume +35-50% vs winter; ferry capacity strain; Cork-Dublin road congestion; DAFM certification queue extends 1-3 days
examples.recommendedAction: Pre-book ferry capacity by March for Jun-Sep; add DAFM cert lead time from April; pre-position UK buffer stock by May; secure cold store allocation for peak

examples.name: Irish Sea Storm Season (Oct-Mar)
examples.description: Irish Sea experiences 3-8 significant storm events per winter (Oct-Mar) causing ferry cancellations. Named Atlantic storms (Storm Eowyn, Storm Herminia type) cause 24-48 hour closures. Post-storm trailer backlogs take 1-2 days to clear. Pattern well-established and predictable in aggregate, though individual storm timing varies by 5-7 days forecast accuracy.
examples.leadIndicators: Met Eireann 5-day marine forecast; named storm warnings from Met Office/Met Eireann; ferry operator advance cancellation notices
examples.typicalImpact: 12-48 hour sailing cancellations; 200-400 trailer backlog at Dublin Port; 1-2 day post-storm clearance; customer delivery failure for time-critical dairy
examples.recommendedAction: Monitor Met Eireann marine from October; pre-position 3-day buffer at Daventry DC; activate NI land bridge routing protocol when amber warning issued; Rosslare-Pembroke as secondary route (more sheltered)

examples.commodity: Irish butter (Kerrygold 227g blocks x 4,800 cases) and cheddar cheese (mature, 2.5kg blocks x 800 cases)
examples.quantity: 24 tonnes (1 x reefer trailer -- mixed dairy load)
examples.shelfLifeRemaining: 85 days (butter 180-day shelf; cheese 120-day shelf -- ample remaining)
